revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安斗智徳]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q83680939|qid=Q83680939|1=Ato no Ōtari}}; {{ill|Tamayorigozen|qid=Q27917346|1=Tamayori Gozen}}}}
== Overview == '''Ato no Sukune''' (安斗 智徳) is a figure from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period|1=Asuka period}}. Also written as '''Ato no Chitoku''', his clan name is also rendered as “Ato” (阿刀), later changed to the 【ill|Shukuné|ja|宿禰】 surname. He held the court Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. == Biography == During the 【ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱】, he followed Prince Ōhito (later known as {{ill|Tenmu Tennō|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}) and later composed the "Ato no Sukune Diary"—a record considered a memoir that contributed to later historical compilations. == Official Positions == According to the "Rokkoku-shi," he was demoted from the Rank of official during the war but was later restored; on January 11 in the first month of the {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|qid=Q1373026|lt=Wadō Era|1=Wadō (era)}} (708), he was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Hōga Toshio|title=Ancient Genealogies of Japanese Clans|year=1986|qq=}} == External Links == * {{ill|Japanese Clan Records|ja|氏族伝承資料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Clan Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{translated page|ja|安斗智徳|version=100494976|comment=Imported full ja history}}
